etymology.description: 又(又) (yòu, 'hand') + 豕(豕) (shǐ, 'boar') with capturing tool 𰅱 -> hand using tool to hunt boar -> 𰅱 omitted, 口/甘 added redudantly -> modern 敢
etymology.interpretation: The character 敢(gǎn, 'dare') evolved from an original depiction of a hand with a tool hunting a boar, signifying bravery or audacity. Over time, a capturing tool component was dropped, and minor strokes or components were added, resulting in the modern form.

examples.chinese: 他敢于挑战权威(他敢於挑戰權威)。
我不敢一个人走那条路(我不敢一個人走那條路)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ bù gǎn yī gèrén zǒu nà tiáo lù.
examples.english: I don't dare to walk that road alone.

examples.chinese: 如果有人敢欺负你(如果有人敢欺負你)，就告诉我(就告訴我)。
examples.pinyin: Rúguǒ yǒurén gǎn qīfù nǐ，jiù gàosù wǒ。
examples.english: If anyone dares to bully you, just tell me.
